scope: A group second-round forecast audit and parameter updates for future match modeling.

audit.direction: missed draw lean; Mexico single-win probability was too low
audit.score: near miss; one-goal, low-event script was close
audit.script: Mexico initiative and risk-management script hit; Korea final-third quality was overestimated

audit.direction: missed Czechia win lean; draw protection was correct but underweighted
audit.score: Czechia one-goal output hit; South Africa late goal path was too low
audit.script: Czechia set-piece/early-lead path hit; game-management penalty was too small

parameter_updates.new: +3.5pp Mexico win probability, conditional on lineup motivation
parameter_updates.reason: Mexico won despite 42.4% possession; home control showed through pressure, key moments, and game management rather than ball share.
parameter_updates.applies_to: Mexico home matches against technical or possession-heavy opponents
parameter_updates.round3_constraint: If Mexico rotate heavily after qualifying, cap at +1pp.

parameter_updates.reason: Mexico converted another low-event match into a controlled one-goal win.
parameter_updates.applies_to: Expected total goals <= 2.35 when Mexico are not an underdog

parameter_updates.new: -3pp South Korea win probability and -0.08 xG
parameter_updates.reason: South Korea had 57.6% possession but only two shots on goal against Mexico.
parameter_updates.applies_to: South Korea versus physical, compact, or disciplined defensive blocks

parameter_updates.reason: Possession volume did not translate into sufficient shot quality.
parameter_updates.applies_to: South Korea possession >=55% with weak shot-on-goal or box-entry indicators

parameter_updates.reason: Czechia again failed to turn a lead/control spell into a win, after also losing from a favorable position against South Korea.
parameter_updates.applies_to: Czechia when leading or expected to defend a narrow advantage

parameter_updates.reason: South Africa kept the match alive and equalized late through Teboho Mokoena's penalty.
parameter_updates.applies_to: South Africa trailing by one goal or level late in low-event survival matches

parameter_updates.reason: Two teams under elimination pressure did not become an open shootout; caution preserved the draw path.
parameter_updates.applies_to: Both teams on 0-1 points where a loss is highly damaging

parameter_updates.reason: Low-event matches require preserving draw and late-event tails instead of over-following the market favorite.
parameter_updates.applies_to: Expected total goals <= 2.35 with unstable finishing indicators

parameter_updates.new: -1pp for Mexico-like validated host signals; keep -4pp for non-host public favorites
parameter_updates.reason: Mexico's public/host signal had real match conversion value; mechanical anti-favorite discount was too harsh.
parameter_updates.applies_to: Strong host signal plus repeated low-event win conversion

model_lessons: Mexico's control should be modeled through key-moment conversion and game management, not possession share.
South Korea possession needs a final-third quality discount against physical compact blocks.
Czechia's set-piece and aerial edge must be separated from its ability to close games.
South Africa remain limited in open-play attack but should retain a late draw path in survival games.
Low-event matches can amplify either a host's 1-0 conversion or an underdog's late equalizer; both tails must remain live.
handoff_to_round3: Mexico's qualification creates a new rotation/motivation variable before modeling Mexico vs Czechia.
South Korea should remain stronger than South Africa on baseline quality, but finishing-quality and physical-block discounts must be checked.
Czechia urgency against Mexico raises attacking volume but also exposes game-management and transition risk.
South Africa's draw/non-loss tail against South Korea should be higher than after round one.
